steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to explain what makes the neon sculpture glow — that requires a cause or mechanism, not a description of appearance or manufacturing steps.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Dunn's 2023 sculpture spells out the phrase in glowing white neon lights": describes what the sculpture looks like, not what causes the glow. ✗
  • Choice B — "The glass tubes have been heated and molded": covers part of the manufacturing process (shaping the tubes), but heating and molding don't make the gas glow. ✗
  • Choice C — "A glow emanates from Dunn's sculpture, which is made of glass tubes": restates that the sculpture glows without explaining why. ✗
  • Choice D — "An electrical current passes through the gas-filled tubes, making them glow white": directly states the cause (electrical current through gas) and the effect (glow), matching the note "An electrical current is passed through the gas to make it glow." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
